content.problemStatement:
Welcome! This is your very first HTML page.\
At this point, you don't need to worry about understanding everything - just focus on seeing how an HTML document is structured.
We'll break down every part of this in upcoming lessons - one step at a time.

 **What This Page Does**
* It displays a **simple Text** that says "Hello, World!"
* It includes the **basic building blocks** used in every HTML page
* It follows a clear structure with opening and closing tags


**You don't need to memorize anything yet. Just know that every HTML page starts with a structure - and this is what you've just seen.**

---


  
  


**View the complete site by launching it in a new tab.**

**Click the Run button to see your first HTML website in action.**
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 100$
- $1 \leq N \leq 100$
- $1 \leq M \leq N\cdot(N-1)/2$
- $1 \leq u_i, v_i \leq N$
- $u_i \neq v_i$ for each $1 \leq i \leq M$.
- The sum of $N$ over all test cases won't exceed $100$.
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of test cases.
- Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.
    - The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers $N$ and $M$ — the number of vertices and edges of the graph, respectively.
    - The next $M$ lines describe the edges. The $i$-th of these $M$ lines contains two space-separated integers $u_i$ and $v_i$, denoting an edge between $u_i$ and $v_i$.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the number of good subgraphs of $G$, modulo $10^9 + 7$.
